name: West Grimstead
overview: West Grimstead is a small ancient village in the civil parish of Grimstead, on the River Dun about 4.5 miles south-east of Salisbury; with neighbouring East Grimstead it makes up Grimstead parish, which had a population of 534 in 2011. The name derives from the Old English for 'green homestead' and the village was recorded in the Domesday Book with a mill. Its Grade II*-listed church of St John has parts dating from around 1300 with an early-18th-century brick tower, and much of the village was part of large estates (Ilchester, then Radnor/Longford) until sold to sitting tenants in 1916.
character: A peaceful, historic village strung along quiet lanes near the River Dun, centred on its old church and village hall and surrounded by farmland.
amenities: Village hall (built 1912-13) and St John's Church are the focus of village life
The village shop, pub and Methodist chapel have all closed (chapel converted to housing)
Nearest pubs and shops in neighbouring Alderbury and Whaddon

schools.name: Alderbury and West Grimstead CofE Primary School

commutes.notes: From Dean station, around 3 miles east, on the Salisbury-Southampton line

prices.summary: A small village with a mix of older cottages and a later 20th-century estate; open-market sales are relatively infrequent. Use the SP5 sold-price record for context.

thingsToDo: Riverside and field walks along the River Dun
Visit the historic St John's Church
Walking towards the Clarendon estate and Clarendon Way
Easy access to Salisbury attractions - the Cathedral and Old Sarum

placesToEat.name: Green Dragon, Alderbury
placesToEat.notes: ~1.5 mi west in Alderbury; Grade I listed 14th-century inn, Dickens's 'Blue Dragon' in Martin Chuzzlewit

pros: Quiet, historic village very close to Salisbury
Attractive Grade II* church and an active village hall
Riverside and farmland countryside on the doorstep
cons: No shop, pub or school within the village itself
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a quiet, historic village within a short drive of Salisbury and happy to travel for shops, schooling and a pub.

sources.label: West Grimstead, St John - The Clarendon Churches
CAMRA - Green Dragon, Alderbury (Grade I listed inn)
